ticket.subject: FedEx REST API Migration - Shopify App Help
ticket.raw_subject: FedEx REST API Migration - Shopify App Help
ticket.description:
Hello PluginHive Team,

I received your email regarding the FedEx REST API migration for our Shopify store.

I checked the PH Multi-Carrier Shipping Solution app and noticed that our FedEx account is currently connected using the Web Services credentials (Meter Number, Web Services Key, etc.), which I understand are part of the legacy API.

Could you please help us migrate the FedEx connection to the new REST API to ensure everything continues working after the March 31, 2026 deadline?

Our store is currently live and using FedEx for shipping labels, so we want to make sure the migration is done correctly without disrupting shipping.

Please let me know the safest way to proceed or if you can assist with the migration from your side.

Thank you for your help.

Best regards

Grape Bottle

comments.body:
Hello Pierre,

We had to conclude the meeting as we did not hear from you.

We understand if you have been occupied. If you would like to reconnect with us, please CLICK HERE to book a slot for the call as per your next availability. We kindly request your presence on the call without fail.

To switch to the **FedEx REST API**, please follow the steps below:

Go to **App → Settings → Add Carrier → Select FedEx (Second Option) → Add Account**. Then connect your FedEx account by entering the required FedEx account details and selecting the appropriate validation type. We recommend choosing the **Invoice validation option**.

Before proceeding with the migration, please ensure the following:

- Ensure all previously generated labels have been printed.
- Ensure pickups have been requested for those shipments.
- If any labels need to be canceled, please cancel them before switching to the FedEx REST API.

To switch to the FedEx REST API, please follow the steps below:
 
Go to App → Settings → Add Carrier → Select FedEx (Second Option) → Add Account. Then connect your FedEx account by entering the required FedEx account details and selecting the appropriate validation type. We recommend choosing the Invoice validation option.

Before proceeding with the migration, please ensure the following:

comments.body:
Need assistance from L3 team:

**Case:** Adult Signature missing on FedEx labels

**Details:**
Customer was using MCSL from April 2024 and was able to generate FedEx labels with Adult Signature. However, after migrating to FedEx REST API, the labels are no longer reflecting Adult Signature.

We have verified the following:

1. Automation rules are updated with Adult Signature enabled
2. Products were updated via CSV with Adult Signature
3. In “Edit Special Features,” Adult Signature is shown as selected

However, the issue persists:

- When labels are generated, Adult Signature is not applied
- In request/response, it shows **service default**
- In product settings, “Delivery Confirmation” shows enabled, but **FedEx® Delivery Signature Options(Rest) is None**

It appears that the **Delivery Confirmation field in the CSV may not be aligned with FedEx REST API**, and may be mapped for Web Services instead.

Could you please review and provide your input?

If possible, please assist in updating all products with:

- Delivery Confirmation = True
- Adult Signature

Expected results - generate FedEx labels with adult signature required service

**Store Details:**

- Store URL: grapebottle.myshopify.com
- Account ID: cbbf7ccb-3c7e-41c8-8018-b24277bba64d

Short recording video -  https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bSuoYeGJgRYdvc2WQQQr1WQMpwwU_Bq3/view?usp=sharing

Hi Manohar,

Thank you for your response.

I believe there may have been a misunderstanding, so I’d like to clarify the issue in detail.

For order #9866, the customer selected and paid for **FedEx Express Saver** at checkout. However, when I attempted to generate the label through the PluginHive app, I encountered a **“packaging type error”** (I had shared a screenshot earlier).

I then tried alternative services:

- **FedEx 2Day®** → same packaging type error
- **FedEx 2Day® A.M.** → label generated successfully, but the cost was approximately $20 higher than what the customer paid

Using the higher-cost service would have resulted in a loss, so it was not a viable option.

As a workaround, I cancelled the shipment in the PluginHive app and generated the label directly through Shopify. The label was created successfully using **FedEx Express Saver**, with no errors.

So the core issue is:
👉 Why was the PluginHive app unable to generate a **FedEx Express Saver** label due to a packaging type error, while Shopify was able to generate the same label without any issue?

I would appreciate it if you could investigate this further and identify the root cause, as this can impact order processing and lead to delays.

I will also schedule a call to discuss this in detail so we can prevent this issue from occurring in the future.

Looking forward to your findings.

Thank you
